name: InstaLend
description_short: InstaLend is a private real estate lender offering hard money and bridge loans for fix-and-flip, rental, and new construction projects with fast approval and minimal paperwork.
description_long:
InstaLend operates as a private lending partner specializing in real estate investment property financing. The company positions itself as an alternative to traditional banks, focusing on asset-based lending rather than income verification. Founded to serve real estate operators and investors, InstaLend emphasizes speed, transparency, and reliability in loan origination and closing.

The company offers five primary loan products: Fix & Flip Loans (up to 90% of purchase plus 100% of rehab costs), Rental Loans (up to 30-year terms for long-term holds), Bridge Loans (12-month short-term financing), Single Family Rental Loans, Multi-Family Bridge Loans, Multi-Family Term Loans, and Ground Up/New Construction Loans. Loan amounts start at $50,000–$75,000 depending on product. Interest rates range from 6.5% (rental loans) to 9.5%+ (fix-and-flip and bridge loans). The company services borrowers across all 50 U.S. states and advertises same-day loan commitments with closing within one week of appraisal receipt.

InstaLend differentiates itself through several operational features: no upfront application fees, no prepayment penalties on most products, asset-based underwriting (no income verification required), minimal paperwork, and a stated commitment to reliable closing attendance. The company explicitly markets to both first-time investors and experienced operators, with the exception of Ground Up Loans, which require prior construction experience or contractor licensing. They highlight real property portfolio examples and case studies on their website.

This lender is best suited for real estate investors needing rapid capital access and willing to accept higher interest rates in exchange for speed and flexibility. However, prospective borrowers should note that rates are significantly higher than traditional mortgages, prepayment penalties apply to rental loans, and minimum FICO requirements of 660 still exist despite being "asset-based." This is non-conforming financing designed for investment property, not primary residences.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Same-day loan commitments and closing within one week of appraisal receipt
No upfront application fees to begin loan processing
No prepayment penalties on Fix & Flip, Bridge, and Ground Up loans, allowing early payoff without additional costs
Asset-based lending model eliminates income verification requirements
Serves all 50 U.S. states with consistent lending availability
Offers specialized programs for multiple investor profiles (first-time flippers, experienced operators, new construction)
cons: Interest rates are significantly higher than traditional mortgages (6.5%–9.5%+), reflecting hard money risk pricing
Rental loans include prepayment penalties, restricting early payoff flexibility on long-term products
Minimum FICO score requirement of 660 still applies despite asset-based lending focus
Ground Up/New Construction loans require prior construction experience or contractor licensing, excluding less-experienced builders
Limited transparency on exact rate determination, LTV calculations, or fees beyond stated ranges
best_for: Real estate fix-and-flip investors needing rapid capital deployment for time-sensitive acquisitions
Rental property investors seeking long-term financing (up to 30 years) with flexible asset-based underwriting
Commercial and multi-family developers requiring bridge financing or construction funding outside traditional banking
First-time property investors with sufficient down payments but limited traditional mortgage eligibility
diagnosis: InstaLend is best for real estate investors and developers who prioritize speed and operational flexibility over low interest rates, and who have sufficient equity or down payments to satisfy asset-based lending criteria. The primary caveat is that hard money rates (6.5%–9.5%+) are substantially higher than traditional mortgages, making this financing model suitable only for short-term flips or investments with sufficient profit margins to justify the cost.
services: Fix & Flip Loans (up to 90% purchase + 100% rehab costs, 12-month term)
Single Family Rental Loans (up to 30-year terms, 6.5%+ rates)
Multi-Family Term Loans (long-term multi-unit property financing)
Bridge Loans (short-term refinance and cash-out options)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
